query: I’ve just completed the payment for the overdue bill. What should I do next to get my service restored?

state.history_summary:
assistant: Hi! How can I help you today?
user: The user reports a phone showing No Service for several hours.
assistant: The assistant identifies line 555-123-2002 and finds it is suspended.
assistant: The assistant checks billing and explains that service is suspended because of an overdue $65.00 bill.
assistant: The assistant sends a payment request to resolve the suspension.
user: The user says the bill payment has now been completed and asks what to do next to restore service.

candidate_answers.text: Thanks — now that the overdue payment is complete, I should verify the bill state and restore the suspended line so your service can come back online.
The visible blocker is line suspension due to nonpayment; after the user completes payment, the next grounded system action is to resume the line rather than start device troubleshooting.

provenance.source_type: manual annotation from real tau2 episode
provenance.decision_rule: Choose a state where a billing blocker has just been cleared and the next highest-value action is to reactivate the line before any handset-level diagnostics continue.
provenance.scoring_method: Manual one-step proxy scoring from the visible telecom state. Scores favor resume_line because line suspension is the grounded root cause already established in the conversation, and the user has just finished paying.
notes: history_summary stops before the bill recheck and line-resume tool outputs
best_action is resume_line even though rechecking bills is somewhat plausible, because the user has already stated payment completion and the episode proceeds directly to line restoration
this sample diversifies telecom best actions into service-restoration rather than data troubleshooting
